summary: A production agentic AI shopping assistant built with LangChain and Claude that manages shopping carts, searches photos semantically, recommends pieces based on customer preferences, and helps visualize art on walls using ML-powered depth estimation.
description: This is a full-featured AI sales agent deployed on a live e-commerce store. It uses LangChain for agent orchestration and Claude (Anthropic) as the LLM backbone. The agent can have natural conversations with customers, manage their shopping carts, search the photo catalog using semantic similarity, recommend pieces based on room descriptions or style preferences, and help customers visualize prints on their walls.
agent_capabilities: Natural language conversation with context awareness
Shopping cart management (add, remove, view items)
Semantic photo search using vector embeddings
Personalized recommendations based on space and style
Wall visualization using MiDaS depth estimation
Answer questions about sizing, materials, and pricing
Handle checkout flow and order inquiries
agentic_features: Tool use with LangChain agent framework
Dynamic tool selection based on user intent
Structured output parsing for cart operations
technical_highlights: LangChain agent with custom tools for e-commerce operations
Claude API integration with proper prompt engineering
Vector similarity search for semantic photo matching
MiDaS depth estimation for realistic wall placement
Real-time streaming responses for better UX
problems_solved: Helps customers find perfect pieces through natural conversation
Reduces friction in the buying process
why_this_matters: This project demonstrates real-world agentic AI implementation - not just a chatbot, but an AI agent that can take actions (manage carts, search inventory, generate visualizations) on behalf of users. It shows practical LLM integration, tool orchestration, and production deployment of AI systems.
